title: Black Forest gâteau
subtitle: German layered chocolate sponge with sour cherries, kirsch, and whipped cream
excerpt: A three-layer chocolate sponge soaked in kirsch syrup, filled with whipped cream and sour cherries, coated in more cream, and finished with chocolate shavings and whole cherries. The elements are individually simple; the assembly is what takes time. Use tinned sour (Morello) cherries, not fresh sweet cherries, for the characteristic tart contrast with the rich cream.
sourceNotes: Schwarzwälder Kirschtorte (Black Forest cherry cake) is a German cake from the Black Forest region of Baden-Württemberg. The cake is documented in German baking literature from the early twentieth century; pastry chef Josef Keller is credited with creating an early version in 1915 at his Café Agner. The combination of chocolate sponge, Schwarzwälder Kirschwasser (cherry schnapps), whipped cream, and sour cherries is consistent across German patisserie texts from the 1930s onwards. The recipe entered the British repertoire through post-war German influence and is documented in British cookbooks from the 1960s.

attrs.term: Kirsch soaking syrup for Black Forest

content.text: is what prevents the chocolate sponge from being dry and what gives the cake its defining flavour. Do not skimp. Use tinned sour Morello cherries rather than fresh sweet cherries; the tartness is essential. Whip the cream to soft peaks for the filling (easier to spread) and firm peaks for the coating.

attrs.prepNote: tinned sour (Morello) cherries, drained, juice reserved

content.text: Sponge: heat the oven to 180°C conventional (160°C fan). Grease and base-line the tin. Sift the flour, cocoa, baking powder, and salt. Beat the butter and sugar until pale and fluffy. Beat in the eggs one at a time. Add the vanilla. Fold in the flour mixture alternately with the milk in three additions. Divide between two tins if you have them, or bake in one tin and split later. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool in the tin for 10 minutes, then on a rack.
Syrup: dissolve the sugar in the water over low heat, cool, then add the kirsch. Whip the cream and icing sugar to soft peaks for the filling, firm peaks for the coating.

Assembly: split the cooled sponge into three equal layers. Place the first layer on a board and brush generously with kirsch syrup. Spread with softly whipped cream and scatter half the cherries over it. Place the second layer, soak, add cream and remaining cherries. Place the top layer and soak. Coat the top and sides with the remaining cream. Use the flat of a palette knife to smooth the sides. Cover the top and sides with chocolate shavings. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour before serving.
